fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space; Tree Coverage
fr.considerations: Dense Vegetation; Single Access Point; Limited Visibility
fr.recommendations: Increasing the accessibility and visibility for responders at the property could be accomplished by addressing the dense vegetation and improving the single access point. Strategically trimming or managing the vegetation around the main building and access routes may enhance visibility and allow clearer navigation for emergency personnel. Expanding the single access point, or creating a secondary access, could facilitate quicker and more efficient entry and exit for emergency vehicles and personnel. Keeping access routes and spaces between the main building and detached structures free from obstruction could support smooth staging and movement. Implementing these adjustments may assist emergency responders in effectively managing operations, ensuring they can perform their duties more safely and efficiently when arriving at the property.

wildfire.conditions: Dense Tree Canopy; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Green Space; Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetated Drainage; Dense Vegetation Corridor
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between dense tree canopies, clusters of trees, and structures could reduce fuel continuity and improve property preparedness. Reducing vegetation directly adjacent to the structure and along the access route may further minimize structure exposure and improve firefighter access. Managing the vegetated perimeter and drainage areas, particularly where dense vegetation corridors exist, could decrease potential fire pathways. Maintaining open green spaces and expanding these areas where feasible can serve as effective fuel breaks, supporting long-term property resilience. Ongoing management of scattered trees and ensuring open areas remain clear of combustible materials can help to sustain defensible space and bolster mitigation efforts.
